title: Changing recipe quantities
instruction: Answer these questions about changing recipe amounts using fractions.
context: Victoria Sponge Recipe (makes 1 cake, serves 8): 225g self-raising flour, 225g butter, 225g caster sugar, 4 eggs, 2 teaspoons baking powder, 150ml double cream, 3 tablespoons jam.

questions.question: The recipe 'serves 8'. You need to serve 4 people. What fraction of the recipe do you make?
Which word means the same as 'caster sugar'?
You want to make half the recipe. How much butter do you need?
You want to make a quarter of the recipe. How many eggs do you need?
You double the recipe to serve 16 people. How much flour do you need?
